body.script: const n=document.querySelector("[data-menu-toggle]"),r=document.getElementById("mobile-menu");n&&r&&(n.addEventListener("click",()=>{!r.hasAttribute("hidden")?(r.setAttribute("hidden",""),n.setAttribute("aria-expanded","false"),n.setAttribute("aria-label","Open menu"),document.body.style.overflow=""):(r.removeAttribute("hidden"),n.setAttribute("aria-expanded","true"),n.setAttribute("aria-label","Close menu"),document.body.style.overflow="hidden")}),window.addEventListener("resize",()=>{window.innerWidth>1024&&!r.hasAttribute("hidden")&&(r.setAttribute("hidden",""),n.setAttribute("aria-expanded","false"),n.setAttribute("aria-label","Open menu"),document.body.style.overflow="")}));(()=>{const o=document.querySelector("a.gh-stars[data-gh-repo]");if(!o)return;const a=o.dataset.ghRepo;if(!a)return;const u=3600*1e3,i=`gh-stars:${a}:v1`;function d(e){return e>=1e6?(e/1e6).toFixed(1).replace(/\.0$/,"")+"M":e>=1e3?(e/1e3).toFixed(1).replace(/\.0$/,"")+"k":String(e)}function s(e){const t=o.querySelector("[data-gh-stars-count]");t&&(t.textContent=d(e))}try{const e=localStorage.getItem(i);if(e){const t=JSON.parse(e);if(t&&typeof t.count=="number"&&Date.now()-t.ate.ok?e.json():null).then(e=>{const t=e;if(!t||typeof t.stargazers_count!="number")return;const c=t.stargazers_count;try{localStorage.setItem(i,JSON.stringify({count:c,at:Date.now()}))}catch{}s(c)}).catch(()=>{})})();

div.p: The open platform for running AI agents
div.h1:
Run open agents without worrying
about agent infrastructure.
div.p: Host OpenClaw, OpenClaw + GBrain, NanoClaw, Anton, Hermes, and other open-source agents with the infrastructure they need to work with real tools, data, credentials, and models.

div.div: The agent UI for knowledge workers — brief the work, watch it run, collect the artifacts.

div.p: Routes each step to the right LLM by cost, latency, and capability.
Protected reproducible execution environment.
Long-term, cross-run recall — facts, preferences, prior outcomes.

div.div: Encrypted secrets store — agents never see raw credentials.

div.h2: Built for real work, not another chat box.
div.p: All that infrastructure needs a front door. CoWork is the agent UI for knowledge workers — a real workspace, not a chat window. Brief the work, watch every step, and collect the artifacts.

ul.li: Connect data and tools through a credentials vault
Choose models from a dropdown in settings — no API keys to wire up for every LLM
Turn agent output into artifacts you can publish and share
One workspace for every agent — start here, switch the agent underneath

img.alt: The CoWork workspace — projects, scheduled tasks, live artifacts, connected data, memories, and skills.

div.h2: Bring the agents you want to run.
div.p: MindsHub is built as a home for open agents and the builders who want control, inspectability, portability, and model choice. Run agents like OpenClaw, OpenClaw + GBrain, NanoClaw, Anton, and Hermes with shared infrastructure instead of stitching it together yourself.

img.p: Run OpenClaw with the infrastructure it needs for tools, credentials, models, data, and persistence.
OpenClaw with a brain that compounds. Memory survives across sessions; the agent gets sharper week over week.
Move lightweight agent experiments from local runs to managed, reliable execution.
The open-source AI coworker. Describe the outcome you need and get a finished artifact back.
Host Hermes alongside your other open agents and connect it to the systems it needs to work.

div.h2: Bring the right model to every agent task.
div.p: MindsHub gives open agents a Model Router they can actually work with: use proprietary and open models side by side, route tasks to the best provider, control cost, and avoid locking every workflow into one LLM.

div.aria-label: Model providers supported by the Model Router

div.p: Agents pick the model that fits the job: reasoning, coding, vision, long context, fast drafts, or cost-sensitive background work.

article.p: Run a strong reasoning model for planning, a fast model for extraction, a coding model for scripts, or a vision model when the workflow needs images and documents.
Experiment with open models while still using commercial providers where they make sense for quality, speed, context length, or reliability.
Swap providers, compare outputs, and route background work to lower-cost models without rewriting the agent every time the model strategy changes.
Model choice becomes part of the infrastructure: keys, routing, provider selection, and ongoing execution live behind the agent instead of inside one-off scripts.

div.h2: Everything agents need for real work, not just demos.
div.p: Real work needs real systems. MindsHub gives open agents access to the infrastructure layer they need to operate safely, usefully, and reliably.

article.p: Use the right model for each task. Switch providers, control cost, use open models, and avoid being locked into one LLM.
Connect agents to real apps and services without pasting secrets into prompts, notebooks, or one-off scripts.
Let agents work with files, apps, databases, warehouses, SaaS tools, and knowledge sources.
Turn local experiments into ongoing workers that keep running after the terminal closes.
Inspect plans, steps, code, outputs, and intermediate state so agent work is understandable and reusable.
Run recurring jobs, daily digests, ongoing research, and operational workflows without babysitting a local process.

div.h2: Start with an experiment. Graduate to a reliable worker.

article.p: Start with OpenClaw, OpenClaw + GBrain, NanoClaw, Anton, Hermes, or another open-source agent your team wants to run.
Pick the LLM that fits each task. Switch providers, mix open and proprietary models, and keep keys managed in one place.
Give the agent access to the apps, files, databases, and knowledge sources it needs — with credentials handled for you.
Move beyond the laptop with hosted execution, persistence, scheduling, logs, and operational control.

div.h2: Run an agent that can actually finish the work.
div.p: MindsHub is built for open agents. For a practical first run, start with Anton: an open-source AI coworker you can hand work to, inspect while it runs, and use with the models, tools, and credentials your workflow needs.

Describe the outcome in plain language. Anton plans the steps, works in a secure scratchpad, and returns useful artifacts — instead of making you copy, format, and stitch the result together yourself.

article.p: Hand off the task in plain language. Anton plans the work and runs the steps instead of requiring step-by-step supervision.
Reports, spreadsheets, summaries, organized folders, daily digests, reproducible analyses, documents, and dashboards.
Bring model choice into the workflow so each task can use the right provider, cost profile, or open model.
A credentials vault lets the agent work with apps, files, data, and tools without pasting secrets into prompts or scripts.
Review plans, steps, code, outputs, and intermediate state so the work is visible, reusable, and easier to trust.
Anton can use memory to preserve context from prior work, learn from repeated workflows, and make each handoff easier to continue.

div.p: Start with a ready-made open-source coworker.

div.h2: A place for open agents to run, persist, and do real work.
div.p: Open-source agents are powerful, but running them reliably should not turn every developer into an agent infrastructure team. MindsHub gives agents a safe place to run — hosting, credentials, model access, tool access, data access, and persistent execution beyond a local experiment.
Start with an open agent. Connect the systems it needs. Choose the model that fits the task. Then run it as an always-on worker with the control, visibility, and reliability technical teams expect.

div.h2: Give your open agents the infrastructure to do real work.
div.p: Host, manage, and run open-source agents with access to models, credentials, tools, data, persistence, and operational control.

a.aria-label: SOC 2 Type II compliant — visit our Trust Center

img.alt: AICPA SOC — SOC 2 Type II compliant

div.p: © 2026 All rights reserved by MindsDB

div.h2: We use cookies and similar technologies
div.p: Essential cookies are always on so the site works. With your consent, we also use analytics and marketing-measurement cookies to understand how the site is used and to measure our ads performance. See our Cookie Policy and Privacy Notice.
Choose which categories of cookies and similar technologies you allow us to use on this site. You can change these choices at any time via the Cookie preferences link in the footer.
Required for the site to work — the anonymous session cookie that lets us measure aggregate traffic. Always on.
Anonymous usage measurement (PostHog server-side). Helps us understand which pages are useful.
Click-ID attribution to ad platforms (Google, X, LinkedIn) and identified events so we can measure ad performance.

California, Virginia, Colorado, Connecticut, Utah, and other US state privacy laws give you the right to opt out of the “sale” or “sharing” of your personal information for cross-context behavioral advertising. We don’t sell your information for money, but we do share identifiers with our advertising partners to measure ad performance. You can opt out here.
See our Privacy Notice and Cookie Policy for full detail.
